jobs.description: I'm looking for an experienced Meta Pixel / Conversions API specialist who can quickly audit and fix active tracking issues on two Meta Pixels for my Shopify stores. This is a small but technical job that requires real expertise. If you know what you're doing, this should take a few hours max. Current Setup: Shopify stores (multiple) Triple Whale for server-side tracking Meta Pixel + Conversions API (CAPI) active Shopify Meta Sales Channel connected Active Issues to Fix: CAPI Event Coverage below 75% ViewContent event: server sending 605 fewer events than browser pixel in last 7 days. AddToCart event: CAPI coverage below Meta's recommended 75% threshold. Meta estimates 56-60% lower cost per result is achievable at 75% coverage. Event Deduplication Keys event_id deduplication coverage currently at 70.85% — needs to reach 75%+. Browser and server events need proper event_id matching to avoid double counting. Event Match Quality Most events sitting at 5.x/10 — need to push toward 7+. Need to ensure all customer information parameters (email, phone, name, zip, country) are being sent correctly with every CAPI event. Potential Duplicate Event Firing Triple Whale AND Shopify CAPI may both be sending events simultaneously. Need to audit and ensure clean single-source server events without duplication. What I need from you: Full audit of current Pixel + CAPI setup across 2 pixels Fix CAPI coverage for ViewContent and AddToCart events Fix deduplication key coverage to 75%+ Improve Event Match Quality Confirm no duplicate events being fired between Triple Whale and Shopify Short written summary of what was wrong and what was fixed What I will provide: Access to Meta Events Manager Access to Triple Whale Access to Shopify admin All current diagnostic screenshots and error details Ideal Candidate: Proven experience with Meta Pixel + Conversions API setup and troubleshooting Familiar with Shopify + Triple Whale tracking stack Understands event deduplication, event_id, FBP/FBC parameters Has fixed CAPI coverage issues before Can work fast — this is urgent Our ad performance is directly suffering from these tracking issues. Meta's algorithm is not receiving accurate and complete data signals, which means it cannot optimize delivery effectively. We are spending budget daily on campaigns that are being shown to the wrong audiences because the pixel is not feeding Meta the correct information it needs to find our ideal customers. Every day this is not fixed is wasted ad spend. We need this resolved immediately so our campaigns can start optimizing on clean, accurate data and deliver the results they should be capable of. Timeline: ASAP — ideally resolved within 24-48 hours Budget: Fixed price — please submit your quote
jobs.title: Meta Pixel & Conversions API Expert Needed – Fix CAPI Coverage

jobs.description: Role Overview The Product Image & Alt Text Specialist is responsible for preparing, optimizing, and maintaining all product images and alt text across the Boxhill website. This role ensures visual consistency, supports SEO performance, and enhances the overall customer experience by following Boxhill’s established standards and workflows. Scope of Work 1. Product Image Management Download and organize vendor-provided images (product-only, lifestyle, and detail images) Edit and standardize images according to Boxhill guidelines: Resize to 1000 × 1000 px Ensure proper framing, clarity, and consistency Convert images to WEBP format for performance optimization Follow correct image sequencing: Main image: best lifestyle image with clear product visibility Secondary image: product-only view Supporting images: details and additional lifestyle views Remove or avoid uploading duplicates, low-quality, or irrelevant images 2. Image Naming Standards Apply consistent file naming conventions: Lowercase only Use hyphens instead of spaces No special characters Exclude variant names from main image filenames Example format: product-name-front-view product-name-lifestyle product-name-detail-feature 3. File Organization & Drive Management Upload all finalized images to the assigned Google Drive folder Organize files using the required folder structure: Vendor Name → Product Name Ensure each product has its own dedicated folder Maintain clean and consistent organization (no mixed files across products) Use finalized, properly named files only (no drafts or duplicates) All uploaded assets must match the submitted Google Sheet entries 4. Alt Text Optimization (SEO) Write descriptive, accurate alt text for all images Include key attributes where applicable: Product type Material Color Shape or defining feature Incorporate approved SEO keywords from the provided keyword list where relevant and natural Ensure keywords are used appropriately without overstuffing or repetition Ensure alt text supports SEO and accessibility best practices Align descriptions with common customer search behavior A structured keyword list will be provided and must be followed for consistency across all products. 5. Variant Image Preparation (If Applicable) Prepare variant-specific images when required: Add variant name overlay (approved font and size) Ensure consistency across all variant images Assist with mapping images to variants within Shopify or related apps 6. Quality Control Review all images and alt text before finalizing Ensure alignment with Boxhill standards and SOPs Identify and flag missing assets, inconsistencies, or issues for review 7. Output Submission (Google Sheets-Based Workflow) All completed work must be submitted via the assigned Google Sheet tracker Each product entry should include: Product Name / SKU Image File Names (final, formatted) Image Order/Sequence (Main, Secondary, Details) Alt Text for each image Notes (if any issues or missing assets) Ensure all entries are complete and ready for upload (no placeholders or missing fields) Submissions are subject to review and approval prior to upload to the website. 8. Source Files (RAW Images – If Applicable) Retain or upload original/raw images when: Cropping, resizing, or major edits were applied Image quality may need future revisions Vendor files are not stored in the central drive Raw images should be: Uploaded in a separate folder labeled “_raw” inside the product folder Kept unedited and clearly distinguishable from final files If vendor source files are already available in the shared drive, re-upload is not required Deliverables Complete and optimized product image galleries SEO-ready alt text applied to all images Properly named and formatted image files Products aligned with Boxhill visual and SEO standards Tools & Platforms Google Sheets Canva (image editing) Image conversion tools (e.g., PNG to WEBP) https://pixelied.com/convert/png-converter/png-to-webp Shared drives and vendor asset folders Communication & Workflow Provide regular progress updates Follow assigned workflows and batch instructions Escalate issues promptly (missing images, unclear variants, etc.) Coordinate with the team when clarification is needed. Performance Expectations & KPI The Product Image & Alt Text Specialist is expected to meet the following performance standards: 1. Productivity (Primary KPI) Target output: 2–3 products per hour Estimated time: ~20–30 minutes per product (depending on complexity) Daily target: 10–16 products per day 2. Quality Accuracy Minimum 90% accuracy rate per product Each product must meet the following standards: Correct image size and formatting (1000 × 1000 px, WEBP) Proper file naming conventions Correct image sequence (main, secondary, details) Complete and accurate alt text No duplicate or irrelevant images 3. Error & Rework Rate Maximum of 1 revision per 5 products Repeated errors or missed requirements may require retraining or review 4. Submission Completeness 100% of submitted products must be: Fully completed in the Google Sheet tracker Free of placeholders or missing fields Ready for direct upload after approval 5. Communication & Issue Handling Any blockers (missing images, unclear variants, system issues) must be: Reported immediately Documented in the tracker or communication channel Avoid delays caused by unreported issues Performance Evaluation Performance will be evaluated based on: Output volume (products completed) Accuracy and quality score Turnaround time Ability to follow Boxhill SOPs consistently

jobs.description: **About Happy Oils UK** Happy Oils UK (happyoils.uk) is a growing UK-based botanical skincare and haircare brand selling body oils, hair oils, face oils, and mineral bath salts. We sell through Shopify online and at in-person pop up events across London and Berkshire. We have 300+ orders, a warm customer base, and two events coming up on April 25 that will generate new leads we need to follow up immediately. **The Role** We need a strategic Klaviyo and Shopify CRM specialist — not just someone who sends emails, but someone who builds intelligent automated flows, segments customers properly, and thinks about the full customer journey from first touch to repeat purchase. This is a part-time ongoing role at approximately 15 hours per month, billed only for hours used. **The Core Problem You'll Be Solving** We have three distinct customer segments that each need their own tailored journey: 1. **Sign up only** — People who joined our email list but haven't purchased yet. They need a conversion-focused welcome flow that educates and builds trust. 2. **Mini/sample buyers** — Customers who buy our mini oils but don't return to purchase full sized products. This is our biggest missed opportunity. We need an aggressive, education-led conversion flow that bridges the gap between the sample experience and the full size purchase. 3. **Full size buyers** — Customers who purchase but take 3–4 months to repurchase. We need usage education flows and replenishment nudges to shorten this cycle to 6–8 weeks. **We also need event-specific flows** — warm, personal follow up sequences for customers who discover us at in-person pop ups and give us their email through our raffle mechanic. **Your Responsibilities** - Audit our existing Klaviyo flows and identify gaps immediately - Build and manage separate automated flows for each customer segment above - Set up event-specific capture and follow up sequences before April 25 - Segment our Shopify customer list by product purchased, purchase size, and acquisition source - Plan and execute a monthly email campaign calendar aligned to our promotions and events - Optimise our Judge.me review request flows to increase review collection - Report weekly on flow performance, open rates, click rates, and attributed revenue **Non-Negotiable Requirements** - Proven hands-on Klaviyo experience — you must be able to show us real flows you have built, not just describe them - Shopify integration experience — you must understand how Shopify customer data feeds into Klaviyo segments and flows - Strategic thinker — you audit first, propose a plan, then execute. We don't want someone who just follows instructions - Strong email copywriting — you write the copy, we approve it. Brand voice is warm, personal, science-backed, and community-focused - Clear communicator — weekly WhatsApp updates on what you've done, what's next, and what results you're seeing - Available to have at least 3 flows live before April 25 — this is a hard deadline **Nice to Have** - Experience with Judge.me or other Shopify review apps - Background working with small product-based wellness or lifestyle brands - Understanding of eCommerce conversion optimisation Hours Negotiable for Setting Up on first month and ongoing regular management.
jobs.title: Klaviyo CRM & Email Marketing VA - Shopify eCommerce (Part-Time, Ongoing)

jobs.description: About Fido's We're a small, family-owned manufacturer of high-quality, ethically-sourced dog products, including bone broth and health supplements made with pasture-raised proteins and organic herbal and mushroom blends. We've built a loyal customer base through Amazon and are now focused on growing our Shopify D2C store. We know our customers love our products (our subscription retention data backs this up), but we haven't had the time or expertise to build the systems needed to retain and grow our customer base the way it deserves. We're a team of two. We care deeply about our brand, our products, and the dogs (and dog parents) we serve. We're looking for someone who will take the time to understand what we're building, not just execute tasks. The Opportunity We're bringing on a Klaviyo email and SMS specialist to help us plug real, identified leaks in our retention funnel and build the lifecycle marketing infrastructure we've never had. This starts as a fixed-price trial project, with the clear intention of transitioning to an ongoing monthly retainer for the right person. Trial Project Scope (~$800–$1,200 fixed price) - Full audit of our existing Klaviyo account, flows, and list health (2,400 active profiles) - Rebuild or significantly improve our welcome series and abandoned cart flow (current conversion rates are below benchmark and we know it) - Build a post-purchase onboarding flow specifically designed to reduce early subscriber churn (this is our most critical gap) - Set up a subscriber cancellation and winback flow - Basic list segmentation - SMS setup and initial integration (Klaviyo SMS or a recommended tool) - Brief written summary of findings and recommendations for ongoing work If the project goes well, ongoing retainer scope would include: - Ongoing flow optimization and A/B testing - Monthly email campaigns to segmented lists - SMS campaign management and growth - Subscription retention strategy (we use Seal Subscriptions but are open to migrating to Skio, Loop, or similar if you recommend it) - Reporting and recommendations What We're Working With - Shopify store with a premium theme and GemPages - Klaviyo account already set up with some history and a couple of active flows - ~2,400 active email profiles - 43 active subscribers with strong long-term retention but a meaningful churn gap at the first renewal - ~$4,500/month in Shopify D2C revenue - Instagram (2,200 followers) and Facebook (500 followers). Note: we have not been posting recently due to capacity - 75% product margin and an AOV of ~$40 You're a great fit if you: - Have demonstrable Klaviyo experience with DTC brands, ideally in pet, wellness, food, or CPG - Understand subscription-based e-commerce and the specific retention challenges that come with it - Can show before/after results from flow rebuilds or retention campaigns - Have experience with SMS marketing (Klaviyo SMS, Postscript, or similar) - Are comfortable working with a small, hands-on brand team and communicating clearly and proactively - Appreciate or are genuinely curious about the premium pet wellness space, our customers are passionate and our brand story matters - Are looking for a long-term relationship, not a one-off gig You're not the right fit if you: - Need a large team or big budget to do your best work - Rely on heavy social content production to fuel your email strategy - Prefer to work with large, established brands only - Can't show specific, measurable results from past email/SMS work To Apply Please include the following in your proposal: 1. A brief description of a Klaviyo project where you measurably improved retention or flow conversion rates, what was the situation, what did you do, and what were the results? 2. Your recommended approach to reducing early subscriber churn for a consumable product brand 3. Your proposed fixed price for the trial project scope above and your target monthly rate for an ongoing retainer 4. Any questions you have for us Generic proposals will not be considered. We're a small team and we read every application carefully, we'd appreciate the same in return.

jobs.description: We are a fast-growing direct-to-consumer brand with significant monthly revenue. We are looking for a highly experienced customer support email agent to manage our inbox on a part-time basis — approximately 3 to 4 hours per day. This is a long-term role for someone who takes ownership, works fast, and gets things done without being micromanaged. --- THE ROLE You will own our customer support inbox from start to finish every single day. Every ticket must be responded to within 1 to 2 hours of receipt and fully resolved same day. You will work directly with the founder and have full access to Shopify and our ticketing platform. This is not a role where you answer easy questions and escalate everything else. We need someone who can handle the full spectrum of customer issues independently. --- WHAT A TYPICAL DAY LOOKS LIKE - Log into the inbox and clear all open tickets — typically 30 to 60 per day, higher during sale periods - Look up every order in Shopify before replying — confirm product, fulfillment status, and tracking before writing anything - Handle order status inquiries and shipping update requests - Manage split shipments — our orders frequently ship in multiple separate packages from different fulfillment partners, and you will need to track each shipment independently and communicate clearly with customers about what has shipped and what is still on the way - Process returns end to end — qualify the return, request photo proof, generate prepaid shipping labels, and log all details in the returns tracking spreadsheet - Issue refunds in Shopify once returns are confirmed received - Maintain shared spreadsheets updated daily — open returns, pending refunds, replacement shipments, and label status - Coordinate replacement shipments for defective or missing items with our fulfillment partners - Handle cancellation requests — attempt to save the sale first, cancel and refund if confirmed - Escalate chargebacks, legal threats, and complex fraud cases to the founder immediately - Ensure zero tickets are left unresolved or unanswered by end of your shift --- REQUIREMENTS — NON-NEGOTIABLE - Minimum 3 years of customer support experience in e-commerce - Shopify experience required — comfortable looking up orders, processing refunds, reading fulfillment and tracking data - Experience with a professional ticketing platform (Gorgias, Zendesk, Freshdesk, Re:amaze, or similar) - Experience generating prepaid shipping labels (FedEx, UPS, USPS) - Experience managing returns and refunds end to end - Experience maintaining operational spreadsheets — returns tracker, refund log, replacement log - Excellent written English — your replies go directly to customers with no editing layer - All tickets responded to within 1 to 2 hours — hard requirement - All tickets resolved same day — no exceptions - Available during US business hours — minimum 9am to 3pm EST overlap required - Stable internet connection — minimum 20 Mbps, must include speed test result in application - Full ownership mentality — you run the inbox, you don't wait to be told what to do --- NICE TO HAVE - Prior experience at a DTC brand shipping physical products - Familiarity with multiple carriers and tracking portals (FedEx, Amazon Shipping, UPS, USPS) - Experience with split or multi-package order fulfillment - Experience handling chargeback disputes and submitting evidence to payment processors - Comfortable emailing fulfillment partners and suppliers directly to coordinate shipments --- HOW TO APPLY Applications missing any of the following will not be reviewed: 1. Brief overview of your e-commerce CS experience — brands worked with, ticket volumes handled, and platforms used (3 to 5 sentences) 2. Two to three examples of real customer support replies you have written — copy/paste or screenshot 3. Description of how you managed returns and refunds end to end in a previous role 4. Your timezone and available hours We review all qualified applications within 48 hours.
jobs.title: Customer Support Email Agent — E-Commerce (Full-Time, Expert Level)

jobs.description: We are building a business automation system for an autobody and spares shop based in Pretoria, South Africa. The system consists of three connected components and we need one developer who can own all three. --- SCOPE OF WORK 1. WhatsApp Private Chatbot — Integrate with 360dialog (WhatsApp Business API) — Build a conversational intake flow: captures customer name, vehicle make/model/year, part needed, damage description, and photo — Real-time inventory lookup (reads from Supabase database) — Replies "out of stock" automatically if part is unavailable — Directs customers to Shopify product URL for checkout — Escalates to human agent when bot cannot resolve — After-hours handling and fallback messages 2. Inventory Middleware Service — Node.js polling service that syncs Posibolt POS API → Supabase every 5 minutes — Handles Posibolt auth token refresh and API rate limits — Writes clean inventory records (SKU, stock qty, price, location) to Supabase — Error handling so a Posibolt outage does not crash the chatbot — Deploy to Railway.app 3. Operator Dashboard (Next.js) — Inventory table: live view, editable stock quantities, add/remove parts — WhatsApp panel: view active DM conversations, flag escalations — "Post deal" button: sends a formatted message to WhatsApp group via API — Shopify order feed via webhook — Hosted on Vercel --- REQUIREMENTS — Strong Node.js (Express or Fastify) — Next.js for the dashboard — Supabase (PostgreSQL + real-time) — REST API integration experience (bonus: Posibolt or similar POS systems) — WhatsApp Business API experience (360dialog or Twilio preferred) — You must have built and deployed a chatbot or messaging automation before — please include examples in your proposal — South Africa-based preferred (same timezone, familiarity with local stack) --- PROJECT DETAILS — Fixed price engagement — Timeline: 3-4 weeks — Milestones: chatbot live (week 3), middleware stable (week 4), dashboard complete (week 6) — All code must be documented and handed over in a private GitHub repo To apply: briefly describe a WhatsApp or messaging API project you have shipped. What was the hardest technical problem you solved on it? We are building a website and ecommerce store for an autobody and spares shop based in Pretoria, South Africa. We need a clean, fast, professional online presence with a fully functional Shopify store connected to it. --- SCOPE OF WORK 1. Marketing Website (Webflow or Next.js) — 5–8 pages: Home, About, Services, Parts/Shop, Contact, Blog template — Mobile-first, fast-loading, clean design — "Shop now" / "Browse parts" CTAs linking to the Shopify store — WhatsApp chat button (links to business WhatsApp number) — Google Maps embed for store location(s) — Basic on-page SEO (meta titles, descriptions, image alt tags) 2. Shopify Store (Basic or Grow plan) — Premium theme setup and customisation to match brand — PayFast payment gateway integration (South Africa) — Product catalogue setup — client will provide product data — Collections by vehicle make / part type — Shipping rules: local pickup + courier options — Order confirmation emails branded to client — Shopify webhook setup (sends order events to our backend) — Basic abandoned cart recovery enabled --- REQUIREMENTS — Shopify experience is non-negotiable — you must have done a full store setup before — PayFast integration experience strongly preferred — please confirm in your proposal — Webflow or Next.js for the website (your recommendation welcome) — Eye for clean, professional design — include portfolio links — South Africa-based preferred --- PROJECT DETAILS — Fixed price engagement — Timeline: 3–4 weeks — Milestone 1: website design approved (week 1–2) — Milestone 2: Shopify store live with PayFast working (week 3–4) — All assets, logins and source files handed over on completion To apply: share 2–3 Shopify stores you have built. Have you integrated PayFast before? If yes, briefly describe how.

jobs.description: We are looking for a technical AI agency to help build a production-ready system for an operations-heavy business with messy, high-variability inventory. Objective Reduce time from: Item received → categorized, priced, and ready for listing What We’re Building An AI-assisted ingestion workflow that can: Process photos (OCR / vision) Extract identifiers (model numbers, labels) Identify or infer product type Enrich data via web search (specs, compatibility) Suggest category, title, and pricing Support human review/approval (required) Output structured data (CSV, automation, or light integration) Must work with existing systems (no full rebuild). How We Build (Important) Modular approach (each step delivers value independently) Human-in-the-loop first (automation increases over time) Leverage existing tools/APIs (don’t build everything from scratch) Simple, maintainable systems to complex architectures Must handle messy/incomplete data gracefully We will be involved in architecture and are looking for an execution partner. Required Experience Inventory ingestion or unstructured → structured data workflows Computer vision / OCR in real-world use Automation in low/no-API environments (CSV, RPA, etc.) Human-in-the-loop system design Integration of third-party AI tools (not just custom models) What This Is NOT Chatbots or customer support AI Generic AI strategy work Shopify/eCommerce optimization Full system rebuild Pure “build from scratch” ML projects To Apply Please share 2–4 relevant case studies including: Problem + approach What was automated Tools used Measurable results (Optional: screenshots or Loom walkthroughs) Good Fit Agencies that build real AI systems for ops workflows Comfortable with messy data + constraints Focused on practical outcomes and maintainability

jobs.description: About Elevate Elevate is an A/B testing platform for Shopify merchants. Our Shopify app lets merchants optimize their pricing, product pages, shipping thresholds, and checkout flows through data-driven experiments. Our platform is growing fast, and we're building a dedicated support team to match. The Role We're looking for a live chat support agent to be the first point of contact for our merchants. You'll handle incoming conversations via Crisp (our live chat tool), help merchants set up experiments, answer how-to questions, guide them through onboarding, and escalate technical issues to our engineering team when needed. This is a Tier 1 support role. You're not expected to debug code or fix bugs. You're expected to be knowledgeable about our product, communicate clearly, and make merchants feel supported. When something goes beyond your scope, you'll create a well-documented ticket for our dev team and keep the merchant updated until it's resolved. What You'll Do Day-to-Day - Respond to all incoming live chat conversations within 2 minutes during your shift - Answer product questions: how to create experiments, configure settings, interpret results, use the visual editor, set up price tests, shipping tests, and more - Guide new merchants through onboarding and their first experiment setup - Perform basic checks using our internal tools (verify experiment status, confirm selectors, check if an experiment is running correctly) - When an issue requires engineering intervention, create a detailed Jira ticket with all context and escalate through our defined process - Follow up with merchants after escalations are resolved to confirm the fix and close the loop - Tag and categorize every conversation for reporting - Participate in daily shift handoffs with your teammate What You Won't Do - Debug code, modify themes, or troubleshoot CSS/JavaScript - Process refunds, credits, or billing changes - Access backend databases or admin panels - Make promises about timelines for bug fixes Requirements - Excellent written English. You'll be communicating with merchants in the US, UK, Australia, and globally. Grammar, tone, and clarity matter. - Experience in live chat support for a SaaS product (1+ year preferred). If you've supported a Shopify app, that's a big plus. - Familiarity with Shopify. You don't need to be a developer, but you should understand the basics: what a Shopify store is, what themes are, how apps integrate, and what a product page looks like. - Comfortable learning a technical product. A/B testing involves concepts like statistical significance, selectors, conversion rates, and experiment design. We'll train you, but you need to be someone who picks up technical concepts quickly. - Reliable internet and a quiet work environment. This is a live chat role, so you need to be online and responsive during your entire shift. - Experience with Crisp, Intercom, Zendesk, or similar live chat tools. Nice to Have - Experience with A/B testing, CRO, or e-commerce optimization - Experience with Jira or similar project management tools - Experience creating support documentation or canned responses Schedule & Logistics We're hiring for two shifts. You can apply for either one, or both if your availability allows. Shift A: Evenings + Sunday (~15 hrs/week) - Sunday, 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM EDT - Monday to Thursday, 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM EDT - Rest days: Friday and Saturday Shift B: Overnight EDT (~20 hrs/week) - Tuesday to Friday, 12:00 AM to 4:30 AM EDT (4.5 hours each) - Saturday, 2:30 AM to 4:30 AM EDT (2 hours) - Rest days: Sunday and Monday Other Details - Fully remote, work from anywhere - Compensation: $5 to $12/hour depending on experience - 30-day trial period with clear evaluation criteria - Room to grow. As our support volume increases, there will be opportunities to take on more hours and expand your role within the team. How to Apply When submitting your application, please confirm which shift you're applying for (Shift A, Shift B, or both) and that the times work for your time zone on a consistent, long-term basis.

jobs.description: We are a growing e-commerce company operating two consumer product brands on Amazon (US, Canada, and EU marketplaces). We are building a centralized data infrastructure to automate reporting and lay the foundation for AI-driven decision making. We are looking for an experienced data engineer to design and build our data pipeline from scratch. This is a Phase 1 + Phase 2 project with strong potential for ongoing retainer work as we expand the pipeline to include Shopify, TikTok, and Meta in Q3. What We're Building A centralized data warehouse in BigQuery fed by Amazon data sources, with automated reporting delivered via Google Sheets and Looker Studio dashboards. Phase 1 — Data Infrastructure Set up Airbyte and configure connectors to pull data from the following sources into BigQuery: Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API) — business reports, inventory, orders (2 separate seller accounts: US/CA/EU and US only) Amazon Advertising API — sponsored products, search term reports, placement reports, impression share reports Keepa API — competitor pricing and BSR tracking Additionally, build an automated email parsing pipeline to extract data from scheduled CSV report emails (from a third-party profit analytics tool) and load into BigQuery. BigQuery must be structured to cleanly separate data by brand and marketplace from day one. This is important — the architecture needs to accommodate future additions (Shopify, TikTok, Meta) without restructuring. Thorough schema documentation is required. Phase 2 — Reporting Layer Connect BigQuery to: Google Sheets — automated weekly and monthly reporting that mirrors our existing report structure Looker Studio — visual dashboards for key performance metrics across both brands Reports and dashboards should cover: sales performance, advertising performance, inventory, and pricing. Specific KPIs and existing report structure will be shared during onboarding. Requirements Proven experience with Amazon SP-API specifically — this is non-negotiable Hands-on Airbyte experience (cloud version) Strong BigQuery and SQL skills Experience building Google Sheets + BigQuery integrations Looker Studio dashboard experience Experience with email parsing automations (Gmail-based preferred) Ability to write clear technical documentation Strong English communication — you will work directly with a non-technical founder Nice to Have Prior e-commerce data infrastructure experience Familiarity with Keepa API Experience with n8n (we plan to use this for workflow automations in a later phase) Experience with Shopify, TikTok Ads, or Meta Ads APIs (relevant for Q3 expansion) Engagement Details Please provide your estimated hours and timeline breakdown in your proposal Strong potential for ongoing monthly retainer (5-10 hrs/month) for maintenance and future data source additions in Q3 Flexible start date To Apply Please include in your proposal: Specific experience with Amazon SP-API — describe a project where you built this Examples of similar e-commerce data pipeline projects Your estimated hours and timeline for this scope Your hourly rate Any questions or concerns about the scope Proposals without demonstrated Amazon SP-API experience will not be considered.
jobs.title: Data Engineer — Amazon E-commerce Data Pipeline (Airbyte + BigQuery + Google Sheets + Looker Studio)

jobs.description: About us We're a growing direct-to-consumer e-commerce brand with a healthy customer base and an established email and SMS setup — but no dedicated specialist actively managing it. We're ready to change that. We want someone who can step in, take ownership, and aggressively close the gap between where we are and where a well-run retention channel should be. What we're looking for We need an experienced email and SMS marketing partner — not just a setup person, but someone who will actively manage, optimize, and grow our retention revenue week over week. You'll be auditing what we have, rebuilding or improving flows, running campaigns, testing constantly, and reporting on results. The first 90 days will be high-intensity: we expect a full audit, a prioritized roadmap, and fast execution. After that, we're looking for a long-term partner to keep performance growing. Your responsibilities - Audit our existing email and SMS flows and identify gaps against industry benchmarks - Rebuild or improve underperforming flows (welcome, abandon cart, post-purchase, sunset, etc.) - Run regular campaigns aligned to our promotional calendar - Manage list health, segmentation, and deliverability - Own A/B testing across subject lines, copy, send times, and offers - Report on KPIs weekly — open rates, CTR, placed order rates, revenue attributed - Keep us moving toward (and beyond) professional e-commerce benchmarks What you must have - Proven experience managing email and SMS for e-commerce brands (DTC preferred) - Hands-on expertise with Klaviyo (non-negotiable) - Strong copywriting skills — you understand tone and can write for conversion - Data-driven mindset — you make decisions based on numbers, not intuition alone - Ability to work independently and move fast without hand-holding Nice to have - Experience with Shopify integrations - Familiarity with SMS platforms (e.g. Postscript, Attentive) - Experience scaling a brand from ~10% to 25–30%+ retention revenue contribution How to apply We screen carefully. Generic applications will not be reviewed. To be considered, your application must include everything listed below — no exceptions. 1. Flow benchmarks (all relevant e-commerce flows). For each flow below, share the real-world open rate, click rate, and conversion rate (placed order rate) that you have personally achieved for e-commerce clients. Do not submit industry averages — we want your numbers. Flows: Welcome series, Abandoned cart, Checkout abandonment, Browse abandonment, Post-purchase / thank you, Win-back / re-engagement Also: Please include Campaign benchmarks. Share your typical campaign-level results across a comparable e-commerce account — include average open rate, click rate, and conversion rate. If you have results broken out by campaign type (promotional vs. content vs. product launch), even better. 3. Portfolio. Share 2–3 e-commerce brands you have managed email or SMS for. We're a small but fast-moving team, so please also mention your favourite colour somewhere in your response, and for each brand include: - Brand name (or category/niche if confidential) - What you managed and for how long - A specific result you're proud of — revenue lift, open rate improvement, flow conversion, or similar - Any visual examples (screenshots, Figma exports, PDFs) are a bonus 4. Availability. Confirm you're available to start within two weeks and can commit to an intensive ramp-up in the first 90 days. 5. Test & interview. Shortlisted applicants will be asked to complete a short skills test before any interview. Be ready for this — it's a standard part of our process

We need someone who understands: * ecommerce strategy * product page conversion * UX / customer journey * channel segmentation * Shopify or similar ecommerce implementation * how to balance DTC with dealer / B2B / OEM audiences on one site ### Scope of work We are looking for help with some or all of the following: **Phase 1: Strategy / Audit** * Review current website and customer flow * Identify where the site is too B2B, too vague, or confusing by audience * Recommend improved site structure / navigation * Recommend how to position TAP RO, ERA Series, and TRE / OEM clearly **Phase 2: Messaging / UX / Wireframing** * Propose revised homepage structure * Propose cleaner product-category structure * Recommend product page strategy for TAP RO * Suggest best way to separate or guide DTC, dealer, and OEM audiences * Help simplify buyer paths and calls to action **Phase 3: Implementation (optional depending on fit)** * Implement changes in WIX Studio or coordinate implementation * Improve product pages / landing pages * Help with ecommerce conversion basics * Possibly support light copy direction and merchandising strategy ### Ideal candidate We’d love to work with someone who has experience with: * Shopify ecommerce sites * DTC product brands * premium or design-forward products * conversion-focused UX * product merchandising * small manufacturers or physical product businesses * balancing B2C and B2B audiences on the same website ### What success looks like A successful outcome would be: * a clearer website structure * a stronger DTC path for TAP RO * clearer separation between consumer, dealer, and OEM journeys * improved product merchandising and conversion opportunities * a site that reflects the brand and product design quality more effectively ### To apply, please include: 1. A few examples of ecommerce or product-based websites you’ve worked on 2. What part of this project you are strongest at: strategy, UX, Shopify build, conversion, copy, etc. 3. Your thoughts on how you’d approach a site that serves DTC, dealers, and OEM partners at the same time 4. Whether you prefer fixed-scope strategy work, implementation, or both ### Bonus if you include: * examples of homepage restructuring work * product page conversion improvements * experience with premium hardware / appliance / home product brands Look forward to hearing from you and working together! Austin

The first is building a shopify store with approximately 10 products over 5 categories just to get us going and then the customer is going to load their own items. I'm happy to use something like dawn theme and just edit it based on the amount of customisation needed. This is for a pet shop, they have minimum amount of requirements needed but some customisation and a really nice looking, polished front end design is essential. Minimum use of plugins to reduce ongoing costs is important I really don't want to have to add 20 plugins to get the site where we want it, but as I said it's a pretty minimal website. Contact us page with contact form, contact details, store open hours and FAQ. Product page simple with product images, Title, Price, Service Reviews (not product reviews yet), Related products, up-sell products, possibly bundle deals, ( Crude example: Buy a fish tank and add fish food and a gold fish for X amount.) Product description, in-stock status, probably 3 static USPs like Same day pickup available, In Stock Now, Best Price Guarantee something like that. I'm looking for someone who has done this before and can hit the ground running with minimal help or intervention needed from me. As mentioned I am far too busy to handhold someone though every step of this project. If we work well together I have much larger project which involved migrating an existing magento 2 store to shopify. Many thanks.

jobs.description: I’m looking for an experienced Shopify designer/developer to build and optimize a multi-brand beauty retail website. This is not a typical Shopify store. The goal is to create a high-end, curated shopping experience that feels elevated, intentional, extremely user-friendly, and highly optimized for conversion. The site should guide visitors through a clear buying journey that reduces hesitation and makes purchasing feel effortless, while also being built to scale into a multi-brand beauty retailer over time. A strong focus on usability, flow, and conversion is essential. --- HOMEPAGE STRUCTURE (Conversion-First Framework) The homepage should follow a structured layout designed to improve product discovery and increase conversions. It should include: • A strong positioning statement that communicates the brand experience and identity • A Bestsellers section near the top to reduce decision fatigue • Shop by Category navigation for quick product discovery • A Shop by Brand section (built to scale as more brands are added) • Visible customer reviews and social proof • A “Why Shop With Us” / credibility section to build trust • A curated section such as “The Edit” to highlight intentional product selections --- MULTI-BRAND STRUCTURE (Very Important) The site must be built to support multiple brands as we grow. This includes: • A dedicated Brands section in navigation • Individual brand pages that include: • Brand logo • Short “About the Brand” section • Product collection • Clean, elevated layout The goal is for each brand to feel intentionally placed and well-presented, not just listed. --- PRODUCT PAGE OPTIMIZATION (Very Important) Product pages should be structured as story-driven, high-converting experiences. They should include: • Strong visual hierarchy so key information is immediately clear • Product benefits highlighted before technical specifications • Visible customer reviews near the top of the page • Clear product highlights (quality, performance, key features) • Frequently bought together / upsell recommendations • Mobile-optimized sticky add-to-cart functionality • Clean imagery and layouts that are easy to scan --- COLLECTION PAGE IMPROVEMENTS Collection pages should help customers quickly identify top products. Product images should support badges such as: • Best Seller • Trending • Editor’s Pick • New These labels should appear directly on product images to guide purchasing decisions. --- UPSELLS & CROSS-SELL FEATURES The site should include clean, conversion-focused upsell features: • Frequently Bought Together (near Add to Cart) • “Pairs Well With” or “Complete the Routine” • “You May Also Like” These should feel natural and elevated, not aggressive or cluttered. --- TRUST & CREDIBILITY ELEMENTS (Essential) The website must feel trustworthy and established, even at an early stage. This includes: • Visible customer reviews and ratings • Clear shipping and return policies • Clean, professional layout • Subtle trust indicators throughout --- CUSTOMER ACCOUNT EXPERIENCE The site should include a clean and user-friendly customer account system. Customers should be able to: • Create an account and log in • View order history • Quickly reorder past purchases • Save items to a wishlist / favorites list • View and track loyalty points The goal is to create a simple, intuitive experience that encourages repeat purchasing. --- LOYALTY / REWARDS SYSTEM (Online + In-Store Required) The loyalty system must work both online and in-store (POS). Customers should be able to: • Earn points from purchases • Track their points within their account • Redeem rewards both online and in-store Requirements: • Must integrate with Shopify POS • Clean and easy-to-use interface • Seamless experience across online and in-store --- BACK-IN-STOCK / NOTIFY ME FEATURE The site should include a “Notify Me When Available” feature for out-of-stock products. This should include: • A visible “Notify Me” option when products are sold out • Ability for customers to submit email (or phone if possible) • Automatic notification when products are restocked --- MOBILE OPTIMIZATION (Very Important) The website must be highly optimized for mobile: • Fast loading speed • Clear product visibility • Easy navigation • Strong visual hierarchy on smaller screens --- PLACEHOLDER VISUAL DIRECTION We do not yet have final product photography. The site should be designed using elevated placeholder visuals, including: • Clean product cutouts • Neutral backgrounds • Minimal, editorial-style layouts The design should still feel premium and intentional. --- BRAND INQUIRY / CONTACT PAGE Create a clean, elevated contact page that allows: • General inquiries • Brand partnership inquiries This should include: • A clean form (name, email, brand, message) • A visible contact email • Design aligned with the overall site aesthetic --- TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS • Built using Shopify Online Store 2.0 • Modular, scalable sections • Clean navigation and structure • Strong focus on performance and speed --- IMPORTANT NOTES • We are starting with limited products but building for multi-brand scalability • The website must be high-converting, visually appealing, and extremely user-friendly • Strong emphasis on customer experience, flow, and ease of navigation • I’m looking for someone who can think strategically and recommend the best tools/apps --- IDEAL CANDIDATE Looking for someone who: • Has experience designing high-converting Shopify websites • Understands beauty / lifestyle audiences • Focuses on conversion, UX, and flow (not just aesthetics) --- Looking for someone who can help bring a high-end, multi-brand beauty retail experience to life — not just build a basic website.
jobs.title: Shopify Designer/Developer for Luxury Multi-Brand Beauty Retail Website (High-Converting + Scalable)

jobs.description: About the Role We are a fast-growing DTC e-commerce footwear brand looking for a reliable, fast, and detail-oriented customer service representative to handle email support. Speed and accuracy matter more than anything. What You'll Be Doing Responding to customer emails quickly, accurately, and professionally across all inquiry types (order status, sizing questions, shipping timelines, returns, refunds, exchanges) Following our established SOPs and response templates to ensure consistency across the team Processing refund and return requests according to company policy, including verifying photo and video evidence from customers before approving claims Helping customers with product sizing questions using our size chart and guiding them to the right fit before and after purchase Escalating complex issues to the team lead when necessary, with clear notes so nothing gets lost Maintaining a high response quality while hitting daily ticket volume targets Flagging recurring customer complaints or trends so the team can address root causes Keeping customer records updated and accurate in our support platform Who You Are At least 1 year of experience in e-commerce customer service, specifically handling email support Fast typer and fast thinker.
